action: Add a fail-closed consumer verifier that regenerates the effective queue from the exact checked-out commit and rejects stale, cross-commit, or tampered artifacts before autonomous task selection.

observed: M-012 established immutable producer-side reconciliation but explicitly left consumer-side verification unresolved.

The new verifier compares the artifact against a queue regenerated from the exact checked-out Git commit, including canonical blob identity, manifest digest, update counts, update blob set, and effective item contents.
The continuity workflow now runs the consumer verifier before uploading the generated artifact.
inferred: Stale, copied, cross-commit, selectively edited, and content-tampered queue artifacts will fail closed when this consumer boundary is used.
proposed: Wire the verifier into each autonomous team's startup before it selects its highest-priority owned item.
superseded: Trusting gate_status and source_commit fields without independently regenerating the commit-bound corpus.
Producer-only queue validation as sufficient evidence for autonomous consumption.
unresolved: GitHub Actions runtime success for the workflow commit is not yet proven.
Peer team startup paths do not yet invoke the verifier directly.

verification: Module, adversarial test suite, and workflow consumer gate were committed and source-inspected. Runtime test passage is not claimed without workflow evidence.
privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata, abstract operational state, synthetic fixtures, and generated evidence only; private chat, health material, credentials, personal identifiers, and unreviewed uploads excluded.
handoff: Continuity Mining should next produce a small startup adapter that verifies operations/generated/EFFECTIVE_QUEUE.json and returns only the highest-priority item owned by the requesting team, failing closed on absent or invalid evidence.
